frameworks.name: AI-mediated deliberation
frameworks.one_line: an AI iteratively drafts and refines group statements expressing common ground

frameworks.name: text embedding analysis
frameworks.one_line: embeddings characterize how successful statements integrate dissent with majority view

frameworks.name: human mediation baseline
frameworks.one_line: human-written mediator statements serve as comparison for AI outputs

core_question: Can an AI mediator help groups of people find common ground in deliberation over social and political issues?
key_assumptions: personal opinions and critiques can be aggregated into a group statement
common ground can be expressed as a single refined statement
manipulations: deployment in lab participants versus a representative virtual citizens' assembly
key_findings: participants preferred AI-generated statements over human-written ones on informativeness, clarity, and lack of bias
discussants frequently updated views after deliberation and converged on a shared perspective
successful statements incorporated dissenting voices while respecting the majority position
results replicated in a representative UK citizens' assembly
interpretations: AI mediation can scale collective deliberation while balancing majority and minority voices
embedding-level structure of statements predicts deliberative success
